title: operator observe without unblocking loop
purpose: A6.3 defines operator observe without unblocking loop for the agent-driven expert system. Pursuit & control plane — autonomous loops until goal verified.
scope: Owns `A6.3` only; siblings under `A6` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §3 — Branch A — Pursuit & control plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#3-branch-a-pursuit-control-plane).
behavior_steps: Operators may read [A6.1](A6.1-notify-dashboard-status-webhook-phase-complete.md) dashboard, `STATUS.md`, journal/progress.md, evidence logs, and platform queue depth while pursuit remains BLOCKED—read-only inspection never clears H2, advances `next_action`, or waives `gates_pending`.

[A5.1](A5.1-continue-resume-pursuit-if-not-blocked.md) and [A5.2](A5.2-continue-not-approval-self-gate-h1-h3-only.md) treat observation separately from resume: opening STATUS to diagnose a failure does not count as Continue or implicit gate approval.

Explicit unblock actions—supplying credentials, answering a `blocking_questions` entry, recording H3 approval or rejection—are distinct journal Resolved Q&A events that preflight must see before [A2.2](A2.2-if-ready-execute-one-pipeline-step.md) may run again.

Under [A3.2](A3.2-goal-autopilot-until-goal-verify-or-hard-block.md) and [A3.4](A3.4-sdk-daemon-run-local-pipeline-24-7.md) unattended modes, the loop stays paused at [A4.1](A4.1-stop-human-h1-h2-h3.md) H2 until preflight passes on the actual fix, not when the operator merely acknowledged a digest per [A6.2](A6.2-notify-digest-on-h2-blocker-not-every-step.md).

If an operator action is ambiguous (e.g. a comment without answering the blocker), pursuit dual-writes H2 with the unresolved question—observation alone never transitions `goal.state` from blocked to pursuing.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
goal_verify passes but H3 rejected â€” goal.state returns to pursuing with rejection notes.
Edge case `A6.3` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `A6.3`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Add `goal` block to state template and journal-keeper dual-write (v2.14).
Implement `scripts/goal-verify.py` stub aggregating task evidence paths.
Document `A6.3` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `A6.3`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: A6.3 defines operator observe without unblocking loop for the agent-driven expert system. Pursuit & control plane — autonomous loops until goal verified. In practice, the system operators may read [a6.1](a6.1-notify-dashboard-status-webhook-phase-complete.md) dashboard, `status.md`, journal/progress.md, evidence logs, and platform queue depth while pursuit remains blocked—read-only inspection never clears h2, advances `next_action`, or waives `gates_pending`, then [a5.1](a5.1-continue-resume-pursuit-if-not-blocked.md) and [a5.2](a5.2-continue-not-approval-self-gate-h1-h3-only.md) treat observation separately from resume: opening status to diagnose a failure does not count as continue or implicit gate approval.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-a 2026-06-28 -->

Operators may inspect dashboards, journals, evidence logs, and queue depth while pursuit remains blocked without that inspection counting as “unblocking.” Read-only observation must not clear H2, advance `next_action`, or waive gates.

This separation prevents accidental resume when an operator opens STATUS to diagnose a failure. Explicit unblock actions—supplying credentials, answering a blocking question, approving H3—are distinct events recorded in journal Q&A. Autonomous loops remain paused until preflight passes on the actual fix.
